title: Distance Coloring

content.problemStatement:
You have NNN stones in a row, numbered from 111 to NNN. You also have KKK different colors with you.

We know that the number of different ways of coloring these NNN stones with these KKK colors is KNK^NKN. But you now add an extra condition:


If ∣j−i∣<K|j - i| < K∣j−i∣<K, then Stone iii and Stone jjj cannot have the same color.
In other words, you cannot color two stones with the same color if they are <K\lt K<K distance apart.

Find the number of different ways of coloring the stones with this extra condition. Since the answer might be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of stones and the number of colors that you have, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of different ways of coloring, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space-separated integers N and K — the number of stones and the number of available colors.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of valid colorings modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we apply the formula. When N <= K we multiply the decreasing sequence K·(K‑1)…; when N > K the answer is K!.
N=3 > K=2, so the answer is 2! = 2.

solution.approach: If N <= K, the first N stones must all have distinct colors, giving a falling factorial K·(K‑1)…(K‑N+1). If N > K, the condition forces the colors to repeat every K positions, so the first K stones must be a permutation of all K colors and the rest are uniquely determined. Hence the answer is K! in this case. Pre‑compute factorials up to 100 modulo 1e9+7 and answer each test case in O(N) for the falling factorial case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * min(N, K)) overall, which is at most O(T·100).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the factorial array of size 101.
